content: Principal Investigators (PIs) must hold a full-time faculty appointment at the level of Assistant Professor (or equivalent) or above at an academic or other non-profit research institution within or outside the United States. The ASTRO-MRA Young Investigator Award is limited to U.S. applicants only, and the UK/Israel-based Special Opportunity is limited to UK- and Israel-based applicants only.
Investigators should be working in an environment capable of conducting high-quality, high-impact melanoma research, but need not be specifically trained in melanoma research.

An investigator may serve as PI on only one proposal submitted to MRA across all award mechanisms in this cycle, including Special Opportunity Awards. Mentors for Young Investigator Award and Dermatology Career Development Award applicants may serve as a PI on a separate proposal this cycle if it represents a distinct hypothesis.

Young Investigators must be scientists within the first five years of their first academic faculty appointment and require a mentorship commitment from a senior investigator. Applicants must be within the first five years of their first independent, full time academic faculty appointment at the application deadline, at the level of Assistant Professor (or equivalent position).

Applicants must designate at least one Mentor who is an established investigator at the same institution to provide support, guidance, and career mentorship. At least one Mentor with expertise in melanoma research is strongly advised.

Applicants who have secured an independent full-time faculty position commencing by June 1, 2024 will be considered, contingent upon a letter from an institutional official or department chairperson confirming the planned date of faculty appointment at the time of application.

Team must be multidisciplinary, consisting of two or more established Principal Investigators (one designated as the Administrative PI), a Young Investigator with complementary expertise, and a Mentor for the Young Investigator.

Applicants for the Academic-Industry Partnership Award must identify an industry partner at the time of application. The industry partner must provide funds and/or in-kind support that matches or exceeds MRA funds for the award period.

Industry scientists may serve as Co-investigators or Collaborators, but may not be PIs. The industry partner will execute an agreement with the awardee Institution and MRA within 90 days if the Award is made, including description of total amount of support (monetary/in-kind).
Applicants for Pilot Awards must be a senior investigator, past the initial five years of their first academic faculty appointment.
Applicants must have a doctoral degree (M.D., D.O., and/or Ph.D.) by the date of award activation.

Supports early career investigators (clinical instructors or new assistant professors). Applicants may hold the title of Junior Faculty, Clinical Instructor, Assistant Professor, or a similar title used by their institution to describe early career faculty. Applicants in a Fellowship position may apply if they are board eligible or board certified in Dermatology, Dermatopathology and/or Micrographic Surgery and Dermatologic Oncology. Trainees (i.e. med students, residents, grad students, or postdocs) are not eligible to apply.

Applicants must designate at least one Mentor who is an established investigator at the same institution to ensure adequate support and career mentorship. At least one Mentor with expertise in dermatology is strongly advised.
Applicants for the UK/Israel-based Special Opportunity must be based at an institution located in the United Kingdom or Israel.

Team Science Award applicants must submit a letter of intent (LOI) to MRA prior to submission of a full proposal (upon invitation). Team Science applications must identify one PI for administrative purposes (the Administrative PI for the proposal). The Young Investigator cannot be the Administrative PI.

Two or more established Principal Investigators (one of whom is designated the Administrative PI), a Young Investigator, and a Mentor for the Young Investigator are required on all Team Science Award LOIs and applications.

LOI: One page maximum that includes a) a description of the scientific aims and translational potential; and b) the nature of and rationale for the proposed collaboration, the specific role of each participant, and synergistic opportunities.
All applications are due by 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time.
Electronic signatures are required for application submission.
List and provide contact information for key persons. Besides the applicant, ORCID IDs are required for the following roles: PI, Young Investigator and Mentor.
Administrative PI (Team Science applications only): Serves as the team leader and primary point of contact for MRA Staff.

Principal Investigator (PI): This is the applicant for Pilot, and Young Investigator applications. For Team Science Award applications, PI(s) share authority for project leadership equally with the Administrative PI.
Co-Investigator: Commits measurable effort to the project and is always designated as Key Personnel whether being compensated or otherwise.
Collaborator: Depending on the role and effort, a collaborator may be designated as Key Personnel (although not required) and may be compensated.

Mentor (Team Science and Young Investigator applications only): Must ensure adequate support and career mentorship. Designating at least one Mentor with expertise in melanoma research is strongly advised. Mentor(s) must provide a letter of support and be designated as Key Personnel.
Young Investigator (Team Science applications only): Work must be integral to one or more aims of the proposal and must be designated as Key Personnel.
Consultant: Provides guidance on specific aspects of the research project. May be designated as Key Personnel (although not required) and may be compensated.

MRA requires that the final, accepted version of any publication be deposited in PubMed Central within 12 months of publication. MRA requires that any data, code, and/or software needed for verification of published research results be curated and publicly available at publication.
Provide a general audience abstract (non-technical) and a technical abstract (2,000 characters, including spaces, maximum each) and keywords.
Awards will not support indirect costs, overhead costs, or other similar institutional charges.

The yearly and total budget caps for each mechanism are as follows: Team Science Awards (including Academic-Industry Partnership): $300,000 per year; $900,000 total over 3 years, Young Investigator Awards: $85,000 per year; $255,000 total over 3 years, Pilot Awards: $50,000 per year; $100,000 total over 2 years, Dermatology Career Development Awards: $85,000 per year; $170,000 total over 2 years.
Provide sufficient budget detail. If more space is required than is provided in the Proposal Central forms (2,000 characters), applicants may upload the budget justification in document form.
For Academic-Industry Partnership applications, the budget should contain all costs, including contributions from MRA and the industry partner, clearly explained in the Budget Justification.

List all current and pending support for the Applicant. For Team Science applications, also list all current and pending support for any appropriate Key Personnel (Principal Investigators and Young Investigators ONLY). For Young Investigator and Pilot applications, current and pending support is only required for the Applicant.
Describe and explain any overlap of current or pending support with the MRA proposal.
Upload an NIH format biosketch for yourself and all Key Personnel listed.

The project description should be 5 pages maximum, including: Background and specific aims, preliminary data, experimental design and methods, statistical plan, figures, and rationale/fit with key criteria, including the potential for clinical impact.
A list of up to 30 references supporting the project description is allowed, in addition to the 5-page project description.

Include letters of support from any Mentor(s) designated in the Key Personnel section of the application. The letter(s) should confirm that the applicant has an independent research program and include a statement about the applicant, the Mentor's role, mentoring plan, the research environment, and sources of institutional support.
Applicant Eligibility Checklist: Required with signature of the Department Chair, Division Head, or Dean.

For Academic-Industry Partnership Award proposals: Include a letter of support from the industry partner, including: i. A description of the total amount of support, clearly indicating the portion that is monetary and/or in-kind, for the full term of the award (which must match the total funding requested in this application), ii. Acknowledgement that if the Award is made, the industry partner will execute an agreement with the awardee Institution within 90 days, iii. Acknowledgement that if the Award is made, the industry partner will execute an agreement with MRA within 90 days.

For proposals involving clinical trials: Attach a brief protocol synopsis (5 pages maximum), along with a timeline and milestones, including IRB and regulatory approval (if applicable), patient accrual timeline, and timeline for completion of analyses.
Provide a brief statement (up to 2000 characters) of how the proposed research project fits within MRA's overall research portfolio.
Before submitting the application, an electronic signature is required from both the Applicant/PI and a Signing Official from the applicant's institution.
Upon acceptance of the award, the PI and their employing Institution will be required to sign an Award Letter indicating acceptance of the MRA's Award Terms and Conditions within 30 days.
MRA requires certification through Proposal Central of compliance with Human Subjects and Animal Care Assurance as applicable.

Ethical/regulatory approval is required before initial payments are made (including local IRB approvals of clinical trials supported by MRA funding). A timeline and milestones must be included in the application package for clinical trials.

For projects including key personnel at other institutions, the PI must verify in advance that funds can be transferred from their institution to the collaborating institution. Sub-award agreements between collaborating institutions must be executed within 60 days of MRA's execution of the award agreement with the applicant institution.
Fringe benefits for personnel salaries are allowable.
PIs will be invited to attend the annual MRA Scientific Retreat.
cfp_subject: Supports pre-clinical, translational, and early clinical research in melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, and treatment. Focus areas include team science, academic-industry partnerships, young investigators, pilot studies, dermatology career development, and special opportunities in melanoma subtypes, brain metastasis, dermatology technologies, immunology, and radiation oncology.
